title: calceolaria ascendens subsp. ascendens
intro: Calceolaria ascendens subsp. ascendens, also known as the Slipper Flower or Mexican Slipper, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Calceolaria ascendens subsp. ascendens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory problems and other ailments.
